# `mise wings login`

- **Usage**: `mise wings login [--token <TOKEN>] [--token-stdin]`
- **Source code**: [`src/cli/wings/login.rs`](https://github.com/jdx/mise/blob/main/src/cli/wings/login.rs)

Authenticate with mise-wings

By default, starts device-code auth and stores a device-bound
credential. `--token` remains as a legacy/manual fallback for
internal debugging.

Examples:

```sh
$ mise wings login
To sign in to mise-wings, open:

https://app.mise-wings.en.dev/cli-device?code=AB12CD34

Enter code: AB12-CD34
Waiting for browser approval...
Signed in to mise-wings as user_123 (acme).
```

Preferred token flow:

```sh
$ pbpaste | mise wings login --token-stdin
Signed in to mise-wings as user_123 (acme).
Set `wings.enabled = true` (or `MISE_WINGS_ENABLED=1`) to start routing tool installs through the cache.
```

## Flags

### `--token <TOKEN>`

Clerk frontend session JWT, pasted from the dashboard's "CLI sign-in" page. Use `--token-stdin` to read from stdin instead of the command line — important so a secret token doesn't land in shell history

### `--token-stdin`

Read the Clerk session JWT from stdin (one line, no surrounding whitespace). Preferred over `--token` because the secret won't show up in shell history
